definition: Paired male glands adding fluid to semen.
Duct carrying semen toward the urethra.
Gland inferior to bladder, around urethra.
Small paired male glands also called Cowper’s glands.
Tube leaving the bladder; also reproductive in males.
Male gonads.
Coiled structure where sperm mature.
Duct carrying sperm away from the epididymis.
External male organ containing erectile tissue and urethra.
Central female reproductive organ.
Lower narrow part of the uterus.
Muscular wall of the uterus.
Canal below the cervix.
Female gonad containing follicles.
Tubes carrying the oocyte toward the uterus.
Sperm-producing tubules in the testis.
Cells between seminiferous tubules.
Paired erectile bodies in the penis.
Erectile tissue surrounding the urethra.
Outer region of the ovary where follicles are found.
Earliest tiny ovarian follicles.
Slightly more developed follicle containing an oocyte.
Large mature follicle with a big fluid space called an antrum.
Large fluid-filled cavity of a mature follicle.
Post-ovulation ovarian structure.
